_descriptor_type: A type of descriptor. One of 'simulation', 'trace', and 'perf'.
_architecture: A name of the available architectures (PARAMS.<architecture>) found in scarab repository, which defines the machine parameters of the default configuration.
_experiment: L1D replacement-policy sweep on SPEC06. Gate experiment for feeder-aware RRIP: measures whether the plain RRIP family (scan/thrash resistant) beats plain true-LRU on the SPEC06 D-cache thrashers (mcf, libquantum, xalancbmk, omnetpp, milc) BEFORE any feeder awareness is added. If plain RRIP does not reduce D$ misses / branch-resolution stall cycles vs LRU here, feeder-aware retention has no base to build on and the win must come from prefetch, not replacement. The lru_feeder_n1 config is the known-losing retention point (N>0 lost IPC on SPEC06 due to long feeder reuse distances thrashing non-feeders) included as a reference line.
_workload_manager: manual: run each exp scenario with all the simpoints at a time, slurm: Slurm schedules simulations based on its policy
_root_dir: Root directory mounted into the docker container home, for instance, the user's home directory
_application_dir: The path to the benchmark application directory used for tracing or simulation.
_scarab_path: The path to the scarab repository that contains the scarab binary used to run the experiment.
_traces_dir: The local directory containing all simpointed traces
_top_simpoint: Run only top 3 simpoints stored in 'workloads_top_simp.json' instead of the entire simpoints in 'workloads_db.json'
_simulations: SPEC CPU2006 integer + floating-point rate suites. The integer thrashers (mcf, libquantum, xalancbmk, omnetpp) and fp movers (milc, soplex) are where any base-policy headroom will show.
_configurations: Sweep of the L1 data-cache replacement policy via --dcache_repl (memory.param.def, Repl_Policy enum in libs/cache_lib.h). No feeder marking except in the explicit lru_feeder_n1 reference config. Enum ordinals: 0=TRUE_LRU, 15=NRU, 16=SRRIP, 17=BRRIP, 18=DRRIP, 19=SHIP. The RRIP family differs only in insertion RRPV; all share the same victim path, which is exactly the seam feeder-aware RRIP (Option B) would hook.

brrip._params: Bimodal RRIP (dcache_repl 17). Mostly distant insertion; strongest thrash resistance.

drrip._params: Dynamic RRIP (dcache_repl 18). Set-dueling between SRRIP and BRRIP insertion.

nru._params: Not-recently-used / 1-bit RRIP (dcache_repl 15). Cheap LRU approximation reference.

lru_feeder_n1._params: Reference: true-LRU + attribution feeder reprieve N=1 (the known-losing retention point on SPEC06). PREREQUISITE: per-simpoint <bench>_<simpt>_braddr.csv from a prior SPEC06 attribute-record pass — present in bld_traces_kmhv3 (dealII, milc, gobmk, cactusADM, ...). Any workload lacking a braddr.csv marks no feeders and behaves as plain LRU.

lru_feeder_n4._params: true-LRU + attribution feeder reprieve N=4. Budget-sweep point: if the N>0 IPC loss deepens monotonically with N, that confirms long feeder reuse distances thrashing non-feeders (retention pays capacity for lines reused past the horizon). Same braddr.csv prerequisite as lru_feeder_n1.

lru_feeder_n8._params: true-LRU + attribution feeder reprieve N=8. Same braddr.csv prerequisite as lru_feeder_n1.

lru_feeder_n16._params: true-LRU + attribution feeder reprieve N=16. Strongest retention; upper end of the budget sweep. Same braddr.csv prerequisite as lru_feeder_n1.

_collect_stats: Stats collection settings for ./sci --collect-stats. configs: optional list of configuration names to collect (null or omitted = all configurations).
_mem: Memory scheduling and collection settings for Slurm jobs.
